note: Post-cut-2 run (review-github split into access + resolution; reviewer templates carry access + 3 folded binding rules). All three cases passed on the first run — the stale-worktree guard added after cut 1 held (checkout verified against manifest-pinned headShas). Zero direct GitHub writes across all runs (mutation capture held).

result.summary: Found one blocking correctness issue: carrier failures can persist a fixed fallback instead of the resolved bracket price.

reviewMutations.body: One correctness issue remains in the carrier-failure fallback; see the inline thread.

comments.body: `currentRateCents()` converts every carrier exception into a successful fixed rate, so `GetQuoteUseCase` never takes its bracket-specific fallback and can persist the wrong price whenever the bracket differs from `fallbackCents`. Return a failed `CarrierRateError` here (and remove the fixed fallback), then add a rejecting-client test that asserts the resolved bracket base is used.

result.summary: PR #2 is correct and adequately tested; no reviewer feedback is needed.
Found a 1,000× volumetric-weight unit error and prepared one inline review comment.

reviewMutations.body: The scope is focused, but one correctness finding remains in an inline thread.

comments.body: The `ParcelDimensionsMm` values produce cubic millimetres, but `5000` is the divisor for cubic centimetres, so this inflates volumetric weight by 1,000× (for example, 400×300×500 mm becomes 12,000 kg instead of 12 kg) and can reject or overcharge normal parcels. Convert to centimetres first or divide by `5_000_000`, and add a test where volumetric weight exceeds actual weight and selects the next bracket.
